product.problem: Developers need a small embeddable cache library that provides predictable TTL expiration and LRU eviction without running a Redis server.
target_users: Library users who need deterministic in-process caching for tests and production services
goals: Expose a small TypeScript API for get, set, delete, has, clear, and stats operations
Provide deterministic tests for TTL and eviction behavior
non_goals: No persistence or disk snapshots in v1
core_flows: A caller creates a cache with maxSize and optional defaultTtlMs
A caller sets a key and later gets it before TTL expiry
Expired entries are treated as missing and removed lazily
When capacity is exceeded, the least recently used entry is evicted
screens_or_interfaces: TypeScript class or factory API exported from an npm package
Public type definitions for cache options, stats, and entry metadata
data_model_draft: CacheEntry: key, value, expiresAt, previous pointer, next pointer
CacheStats: size, hits, misses, evictions, expirations
external_integrations: Optional package manager integration through npm metadata
success_criteria: All public API operations have deterministic unit tests
TTL expiration works with an injected clock in tests
LRU order updates on get and set
Capacity overflow evicts exactly one least-recently-used live entry
constraints: Correctness and deterministic behavior before benchmarks

architecture: Use a Map for O(1) key lookup and a doubly linked list for recency ordering
Use lazy TTL cleanup on get, has, set, and capacity enforcement
Inject a clock function so tests can advance time deterministically
data_flow: set inserts or updates an entry, computes expiresAt, moves the entry to most-recent position, then enforces capacity
get checks expiry, records hit or miss, removes expired entries, and moves live entries to most-recent position
capacity enforcement evicts from the least-recent side until size is within maxSize
dependencies: Node.js test runner or equivalent deterministic unit test framework
edge_cases: Updating an existing key refreshes value, TTL, and recency
Expired least-recent entries are removed before evicting live entries
verification: Unit tests for TTL with injected clock
Unit tests for LRU order after get and set

evidence.title: Answered intake decisions
evidence.used_for: Confirmed TypeScript/Node.js, single-process scope, and deterministic correctness priority.

evidence.title: Redis eviction reference
evidence.used_for: Kept Redis as behavior inspiration while explicitly excluding protocol/server compatibility.
